title: Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier
summary: Train a secondary binary ML classifier (the 'meta-model') on top of the primary 9-dimension signal engine. The meta-model's only job is to predict whether a given primary signal will be profitable or not — it does not pick direction. By combining the primary signal with the meta-model's confidence score, the system can selectively skip low-confidence trades, raising precision at the cost of some recall. Hudson & Thames research confirms meta-labeling consistently improves precision and F1 scores.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Execution Quality & Exit Optimization
